title: quercus cinerea var. pumila
intro: Quercus cinerea var. pumila, also known as Dwarf Oak, is a small shrub-like evergreen tree that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). The leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Quercus cinerea var. pumila include reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as acting as a natural remedy for various skin conditions and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or a solvent to release their medicinal compounds.
